summary: The state’s redistricting board is considering its options after the Alaska Supreme Court ruled its first choice unconstitutional.

title: U.S. Interior Secretary Deb Haaland to visit Alaska this month

title: Alaska Sen. Lisa Murkowski says she’ll vote to confirm Jackson, becoming 2nd Republican to back Supreme Court nominee
summary: Republican Sens. Lisa Murkowski and Mitt Romney say they will vote toconfirm Judge Ketanji Brown Jackson’s “historic nomination” to theSupreme Court, bolstering bipartisan support for the first Black womanto be nominated for the court.

title: Open & Shut: Anchorage gets a new vintage clothing store, a veterinary clinic opens, and Uncle Leroy’s ends its run in Midtown
summary: An auction house, a brewery, an assisted-living home and a physical therapy business also opened, and Sonic starts its car-hop service. An organic bakery in Wasilla closes.

summary: An analyst predicted Musk, the world’s richest man, will eventually pursue an active stake, positioning him to take “more aggressive ownership role” in the company.

title: 6 steps the world can take to halt climate change
summary: The U.N.’s latest climate change report details the massive shifts necessary to cut the world’s emissions. But it also outlines various ways that nations can help hit the brakes on warming.

title: Alaska fishing industry writer Laine Welch ends her weekly report after 3 decades

summary: It was the first in the government’s sanctions initiative to “seize and freeze” giant boats and other pricey assets of Russian elites.

title: An election win so satisfying, Governor Jay Hammond had to write a song about it
summary: Alaska history: Hammond, who defeated Wally Hickel by 98 votes in the 1978 gubernatorial primary, penned “Nip and Tuck” to commemorate the victory.

title: Parts of Northwest Alaska will be closed to nonlocal caribou and moose hunters the next 2 summers

title: With Alaska struggling to hire, state legislators consider revived pension plans for public employees

title: Man in Germany gets 90 COVID-19 shots to sell forged passes

title: 50 candidates: A wild U.S. House race takes shape in Alaska

summary: Ocean warming has put at risk the historic Alaska crab fishery. After a dismal summer survey, state biologists slashed this year’s harvest of snow crab by nearly 90% from 2021 levels.

title: There are new migrants on the U.S.-Mexico border: Ukrainian refugees
